behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech model diarization/label quality (LLM-DEP equivalent for AI/ML). A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide real mislabeling, so mocks can miss the bug (MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow: behavior targets one model decision surface (speaker labeling), not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert speaker labels remain attached to utterances and map correctly to attendee identities used by the summarizer input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is a local parsing/transform rule and can fail within one function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor). No real OS/network/subprocess needed, and no dependence on real model output content. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on parsing extraction logic itself, not module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted transcript text samples into the parser and assert exact structured extraction of assignee and task text fields, including edge cases (multiple assignees, punctuation, partial statements).

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM generation behavior (coverage of assigned attendees), so deterministic fixtures can always pass while hiding failures (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Not Workflow: this assertion is about one model output stage’s content quality, not sequential multi-agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and verify each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'identified decisions', so the test target is routing/mapping logic after identification. That is a local deterministic transform and does not require real infra or model judgment. Rejected Agentic because model decision quality is explicitly out of scope in this behavior phrasing.
behaviors.test_description: Pass pre-identified decision objects through the routing mapper and assert they populate only the `decisions` schema fields and never narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output content quality: extracting salient decisions and placing them correctly. A mocked model response would hide real misses/misplacement (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow: single summarization decision surface is sufficient for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real transcripts containing explicit decisions to the live summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (per-segment transcription/summarization and merge), plus correctness of handoff across stages (MULTI-STEP, integration-brake d). Stubs can validate mechanics but miss coherence failures caused by real model outputs across segment boundaries. Rejected lower levels because behavior explicitly includes sequential segmented processing and coherent merged output.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify sequential segment processing and final merge, then evaluate that cross-segment action items/decisions are consistently represented in one coherent structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage agent pipeline with real audio, real transcription model output, and real summarization model output, requiring sequential agentic correctness. It exceeds System because success depends not just on infra plumbing but on chained AI outputs across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like E2E run using a real meeting recording: upload, transcription API call(s), summarization call(s), and final structured delivery, asserting successful completion and valid schema output without human fixes.
